METADATA.purpose: Complete combat optimization system: Limb destruction → Endurance dominance → Grappling submission
METADATA.context: 3 months sober, intensive outpatient treatment, seeking complete physical + neurobiological transformation

ABSTRACT_CONCEPT.title: METABOLIC ASYMMETRY DOMINANCE: A Systems-Based Combat Victory Framework
ABSTRACT_CONCEPT.thesis:
You do not win through luck or superior technique alone. You win through systematic 
exploitation of three physiological asymmetries that compound across 10-15 minutes:

1. DAMAGE ASYMMETRY: Repeated strikes to opponent's arms cause 30-50% greater muscle 
   damage than leg strikes (arms are more eccentric-damage-susceptible). Their grip 
   degrades 16-25%. Yours is trained 3x/week and remains constant.

2. METABOLIC ASYMMETRY: Your HIIT-trained metabolism maintains 95% power output in 
   round 3. Opponent's standard conditioning allows only 60-70% power output. That is 
   a 25-35 point automatic advantage if you stay conscious to exploit it.

3. NEUROLOGICAL ASYMMETRY: Fatigue doesn't just make muscles weak—it degrades decision-making. 
   Opponent's reaction time increases 1.5%+, consistency drops 14.7%, striking accuracy 
   -15-25% by minute 6-8. Their brain cannot process your clinch entry. They cannot sprawl. 
   They cannot defend submissions.

Your strategy exploits these asymmetries sequentially:
- Minutes 0-6: CREATE damage asymmetry (strike limbs 40-60 times)
- Minutes 4-8: MONITOR metabolic asymmetry (identify fatigue signals)
- Minutes 7-10: EXPLOIT neurological asymmetry (clinch + takedown when they cannot react)
- Minutes 10+: CAPITALIZE neurological + muscular weakness (back control + submission)

Victory is not random. It is inevitable IF you:
1. Build damage resilience (condition limbs to absorb strikes)
2. Build metabolic superiority (HIIT 3x/week + breathing protocol)
3. Build technical precision (grappling back control 2x/week + submissions)
4. Execute phases sequentially (recognize signals, transition tactics)

This is your competitive advantage: Most fighters are generalists (okay at striking, 
okay at grappling). You are an asymmetry-exploitation specialist. You identify what 
you are superior at (endurance + grappling), then systematically dismantle their 
strengths (arm function + cardiovascular capacity) until they cannot defend their 
own defeat.

This is not martial arts. This is applied physiology + psychology + systems thinking.
ABSTRACT_CONCEPT.key_differentiator: Endurance dominance transforms from luxury to REQUIREMENT. You cannot execute this strategy without 95%+ round 3 capacity. This forces complete life systematization: sleep, nutrition, recovery, consistency. Everything compounds.

priority_1_endurance_foundation.name: Cardiovascular Capacity via HIIT
priority_1_endurance_foundation.integration: Combined with all strength sessions (never separate)
priority_1_endurance_foundation.expected_outcome_12weeks: +34% endurance capacity, +16% dopamine restoration, +400% BDNF elevation

priority_2_striking_power_limb_damage.target_rep_range: 8-12 reps x 4 sets (hypertrophy + metabolic stress + endurance)
exercises: Knuckle conditioning: Progressive impact training (sandbag, rice bucket)
Forearm strikes: Repeated contact adaptation (iron palm equivalent)
priority_2_striking_power_limb_damage.purpose: Arm speed for striking + grip dominance for clinching + submission mechanics
priority_2_striking_power_limb_damage.combat_integration: Damage opponent's arms while maintaining YOUR grip strength
priority_2_striking_power_limb_damage.expected_outcome_12weeks: Grip strength +25%, hand endurance 3x, striking speed +34%

exercises: Core rotation striking drills: Dynamic strikes with controlled rotation
priority_3_striking_power_rotational.purpose: Rotational power for strikes (60-70% punch power) + clinch control stability
priority_3_striking_power_rotational.combat_integration: Power your limb strikes + maintain clinch angle against opponent's escape attempts
priority_3_striking_power_rotational.expected_outcome_12weeks: Rotational power +25%, striking speed +20%, clinch control +30%

exercises: Face pulls: 12-15 reps (shoulder health + rear delt)
priority_4_grappling_dominance.purpose: Back control dominance (CRITICAL grappling position), pulling power for clinch control
priority_4_grappling_dominance.combat_integration: Back control is your endgame victory path: rear naked choke, positional dominance, exhaustion management
priority_4_grappling_dominance.expected_outcome_12weeks: Back strength +30%, rear control endurance +40%, submission success rate 85%+

exercises: Bulgarian split squats: 10-12 reps each leg
priority_5_leg_power_takedown.purpose: Leg kick power for damage + takedown execution drive + base stability
priority_5_leg_power_takedown.combat_integration: Powerful leg strikes to opponent = extended endurance advantage. Takedown drive from strong glutes.
priority_5_leg_power_takedown.expected_outcome_12weeks: Leg power +30%, takedown success +25%, kicking damage +40%

priority_6_support_structures.purpose: Arm acceleration for strikes + shoulder health for repeated striking
priority_6_support_structures.combat_integration: Arm speed depends on shoulder mobility + rotator cuff integrity. Resilience against opponent strikes.

WEEKLY_SCHEDULE_DETAILED.overview: 6 training days, 1 rest day. Each day repeats the same muscle groups to enable mastery progression and protocol consistency.
WEEKLY_SCHEDULE_DETAILED.week_structure: Fixed pattern allows neurological adaptation (same movements same day) + progressive overload (weight/volume increases weekly)

monday.name: Upper 1: Pushing Power + Arm Speed + HIIT
monday.duration: 55 minutes total (35 min strength + 20 min HIIT)

exercises.name: Farmer's Carry (Grip Dominance)
exercises.progression: Week 1: 60 sec. Week 4: 75 sec. Week 8: 90 sec. Week 12: 120 sec.

exercises.name: Overhead Press (Shoulder Power)
exercises.progression: +5 lbs weekly (or +1-2 reps if at weight ceiling)

exercises.name: Rotational Medicine Ball Slams (Core Power)
exercises.purpose: Rotational power for strikes + clinch control
exercises.progression: Increase medicine ball weight by 2 lbs every 2 weeks

exercises.name: Wrist Flexion/Extension Curls (Forearm Conditioning)
exercises.progression: +5 lbs every 2 weeks or increase reps by 2-3

HIIT_block.structure: 45 sec work / 15 sec rest x 18 intervals
HIIT_block.breathing_protocol: Incorporate straw breathing (2-4 min) during HIIT

monday.daily_focus: Pushing power establishes arm speed + clinch dominance foundation

tuesday.name: Lower 1: Hip Power + Leg Kicks + HIIT
tuesday.duration: 55 minutes total (35 min strength + 20 min HIIT)

exercises.name: Barbell Back Squat (Hip Power Foundation)
exercises.tempo: 2 sec down, 1 sec pause, explosive up
exercises.purpose: Maximum leg power for kicks + takedown base

exercises.name: Romanian Deadlifts (Hamstring + Posterior Chain)
exercises.purpose: Hip extension power for kicks + takedown defense

exercises.name: Hip Thrusts (Glute Activation + Power)
exercises.purpose: Glute activation for maximum power output

exercises.name: Grip Training: Heavy Negatives (Grip Strength Peak)
exercises.purpose: Grip strength progression toward superhuman levels
exercises.progression: Add weight every week if possible, otherwise add reps

HIIT_block.structure: 45 sec work / 15 sec rest x 18 intervals

tuesday.daily_focus: Hip power establishes leg kick damage + takedown base

wednesday.name: Upper 2: Pulling Power + Back Dominance + HIIT
wednesday.duration: 55 minutes total (35 min strength + 20 min HIIT)

exercises.name: Bent-Over Barbell Rows (Back Strength Foundation)
exercises.purpose: Back dominance for rear control submissions

exercises.name: Lat Pulldowns (Back Width + Pulling Power)
exercises.tempo: 1 sec down, 1 sec squeeze, 2 sec up

exercises.name: Seal Rows (Back Dominance + Scapular Control)
exercises.tempo: 2 sec down, 1 sec squeeze, explosive up
exercises.purpose: Advanced back control for positional dominance

exercises.name: Scapular Pulls (Scapular Control + Stability)
exercises.purpose: Shoulder blade stability for arm control in grappling

HIIT_block.structure: 45 sec work / 15 sec rest x 18 intervals

wednesday.daily_focus: Back dominance establishes grappling submission position mastery

thursday.name: Lower 2: Rotation + Core Dominance + HIIT
thursday.duration: 55 minutes total (35 min strength + 20 min HIIT)

exercises.name: Cable Woodchops (Rotational Power)
exercises.purpose: Rotational power for striking + clinch angle control

exercises.name: Anti-Rotation Holds (Core Stability)
exercises.purpose: Core stability against opponent clinch escape attempts
exercises.progression: +15 sec hold time weekly, or increase weight

exercises.name: Bulgarian Split Squats (Single-Leg Power)
exercises.purpose: Unilateral leg strength for takedown base + kick power

exercises.name: Knuckle Conditioning / Sandbag Strikes (Limb Toughening)
exercises.weight: Progressive (rice bucket → light sandbag → medium sandbag)
exercises.purpose: Repeated eccentric damage adaptation (build resilience to repeated strikes)
exercises.progression: Week 1-4: Rice bucket. Week 5-8: Light sandbag (20 lbs). Week 9-12: Medium sandbag (40 lbs)

exercises.name: Farmer's Carry Variation: Unilateral (Single-Arm Grip + Core)
exercises.progression: +15 seconds every 2 weeks, or increase weight

HIIT_block.structure: 45 sec work / 15 sec rest x 18 intervals

thursday.daily_focus: Core rotation establishes striking power + clinch control

friday.name: Upper 3: Speed + Arm Acceleration + HIIT
friday.duration: 55 minutes total (35 min strength + 20 min HIIT)

exercises.name: Explosive Push-Ups (Arm Speed Foundation)
exercises.progression: Add weight every 2 weeks (+10 lbs vest) or increase reps

exercises.name: Wrist Flexion/Extension Curls - Speed Variation (Arm Speed)
exercises.progression: +2-3 reps every week, or increase weight

HIIT_block.structure: 45 sec work / 15 sec rest x 18 intervals
HIIT_block.breathing_protocol: Straw breathing 2-4 min integrated, emphasis on maintaining speed

friday.daily_focus: Arm speed establishes striking velocity for limb damage accumulation

saturday.name: Lower + Conditioning: Endurance + Movement Quality + HIIT
saturday.duration: 40 minutes total (20 min light movement + 20 min HIIT)

exercises.name: Light Movement Circuit (Recovery)
exercises_in_circuit: Sled push (if available) or light jog: 2 minutes

exercises.name: Mobility Work (Injury Prevention)
exercises: Hip flexor stretches: 30 sec each side
exercises.purpose: Maintain mobility + reduce injury risk from week's training

HIIT_block.structure: 40 sec work / 20 sec rest x 20 intervals
HIIT_block.breathing_protocol: Advanced IHIT: Combine straw breathing + breath holds (3-4 min total)
HIIT_block.intensity: 75-85% max effort (slightly lower than weekday HIIT for recovery emphasis)

saturday.daily_focus: Recovery emphasis while maintaining metabolic conditioning. Introduction of advanced breathing protocols.

sunday.name: COMPLETE REST DAY
activities: Nutrition: High protein (200+ grams), high carbs (post-workout recovery)
Optional light activity: 20-30 minute walk, gentle stretching
sunday.purpose: Complete nervous system recovery + muscle protein synthesis
sunday.notes: This day is NON-NEGOTIABLE. Recovery is where adaptation happens.

week_1_foundation.monday_friday: Straw breathing only: 2 minutes per HIIT session (during work intervals)
week_1_foundation.protocol: Breathe through narrow straw during 45-sec work intervals (aim for 3-4 intervals per session)
week_1_foundation.effect: Adapt to increased CO2, begin lung capacity expansion
week_1_foundation.notes: May feel uncomfortable first 2-3 sessions. This is normal. Adaptation begins day 3-4.

week_2_integration.monday_friday: Straw breathing 2-3 minutes per session (same as week 1, increase slightly)
week_2_integration.saturday: Introduce breath holds: After 5-minute warm-up HIIT, perform breath hold protocol (see below)

breath_hold_protocol.phase_1: 30 sec deep breathing (hyperventilation to lower CO2)
breath_hold_protocol.phase_2: Exhale fully, hold breath at functional residual capacity: 60-90 sec
breath_hold_protocol.phase_3: Ladder breathing: Allow 10 small breaths while holding (maintain hypoxia)

week_2_integration.effect: Blood O2 drops to 85-90% (controlled mild hypoxia), CO2 adaptation, mitochondrial density beginning to increase

week_3_advanced.monday_thursday: Straw breathing 2-3 minutes per session (maintain week 2 level)
week_3_advanced.friday_saturday: Combine straw breathing + breath holds: 3-4 minutes integrated breathing protocol
week_3_advanced.combination_protocol: Alternate: 2 min straw breathing → 2-3 min breath hold protocol → Resume normal breathing
week_3_advanced.effect: Significant CO2 tolerance improvement, oxygen efficiency increasing

week_4_peak.daily: Straw breathing 3-4 minutes, plus breath holds 2-3 minutes, total 5-7 minutes per session
week_4_peak.advanced_technique: Introduce 'ladder breathing' within straw breathing: Breath through straw, then brief nose pause, repeat
week_4_peak.effect: Peak CO2 tolerance by end of week 4. Significant endurance advantage beginning to manifest in sparring/competition.

weeks_5_12_maintenance.protocol: 4-5 minutes combined breathing protocol per HIIT session
weeks_5_12_maintenance.progression: Gradually increase breath hold duration (add 10-15 sec weekly) and straw breathing intervals
weeks_5_12_maintenance.effect: Continuous mitochondrial adaptation. Lactate buffering increases. By week 12, you are metabolically superior to standard-trained fighters.

DO: Progress gradually (1 week per phase, not faster)
Exit immediately if severe dizziness or discomfort
Practice during scheduled HIIT only, not all-day
Combine with full oxygenation recovery (normal breathing) between sessions
DON'T: Practice before driving or operating machinery (20-30 min minimum after session)
Combine straw breathing + breath hold + HIIT intensity simultaneously at first
Force hypoxia or breath holds if it causes pain (controlled stress, not damage)

BREATHING_PROTOCOL_IMPLEMENTATION.expected_combat_outcome: Round 3 advantage: You breathing normally, opponent cannot catch breath. This is metabolic dominance manifesting.

PROGRESSIVE_OVERLOAD_TRACKING.mechanism: Weekly progression ensures you are always improving, never plateauing

progression_variables.weight_increase: +5 lbs per week on heavy lifts, +2-3 lbs every 2 weeks on accessories
progression_variables.rep_increase: +1-2 reps per week on maintenance weights, progress to new weight when ceiling hit
progression_variables.time_increase: +15-30 sec per week on holds, carries, breathing protocols
progression_variables.volume_increase: +1-2 sets every 3 weeks on accessories, maintain intensity

deload_schedule.week_4: Reduce volume to 50%, maintain intensity (lighter weights, same RPE effort)
deload_schedule.week_8: Reduce volume to 50%, reduce intensity (lighter weights, lower effort)
deload_schedule.week_12: Assessment week: Test 1RM maxes, document progress, plan next phase
